programme.desc: Willing to kill for just $20 and drugs, Joshua Gardner commits his first murder at 24. Even behind bars, he can't contain his rage, brutally beating a fellow inmate to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The horrifying story of a computer engineer who lured a 14-year-old school boy to his flat and killed him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: He portrayed himself as a pillar of the community, but the story of this cult killer was deeply sinister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Shore was a dangerous sexual predator who will be remembered as one of the world's most evil killers.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the woman who was driven into a jealous rage and stabbed her sister to death while her children slept upstairs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Carol Jarvis disappeared, her husband told friends and family that she'd simply gone on a little holiday.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Beltway sniper attacks. John Allen and his teenage accomplice carried out the brutal series of shootings that killed ten.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over six years, Eddie Seda, the copycat Zodiac killer stalked the streets of New York City, in search of easy targets. His cowardly attacks culminated with the ferocious murder of a woman, who had only asked him for a cigarette.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles Albright's entire life revolved around crime. He graduated from being a small-time crook, into a child-molester and then evolved into a serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, Shannon Melendi disappeared while at Emory University. Her disappearance remained a mystery for ten years, until new scientific testing cast a different light on Colvin "Butch" Hinton.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It's usually easy to determine how a criminal entered the crime scene. But in this case, it was far from clear. It looked like the killer vanished into thin air...and perhaps he had.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, college co-ed Jenna Verhaalen was found dead in her Bryan, Texas apartment. Petechial hemorrhages in her eyes indicated that she was strangled.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lives changed in the 20 years following an unsolved murder, and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed a clue no one had seen before.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After inspecting storm damage to a home in Tampa, an insurance assessor simply disappeared. Thirty hours later, her body was found in a nearby river. But the killer had been careless.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When court clerk Peter Porco doesn't report to his work in November 2004, a courts officer is ordered to the Porcos' family home in Bethlehem, NY.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a car was found in a drainage ditch with two bodies inside, a fingertip torn from a latex glove would point investigators to both the crime scene and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lives changed in the 20 years following an unsolved murder, and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed a clue no one had seen before.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Avis Banks and her unborn child were brutally murdered. The body was discovered by her fiancé, Keyon Pittman. When police learned that Pittman was having affairs with other women, he became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A body is found stuffed into a suitcase forcing the police to search though the landfill for another missing victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Volunteers and police spend weeks searching for a missing girl. A psychic later leads them to a field where the body was found.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2006, Texas real estate agent Sarah Anne Walker was found brutally murdered in a model home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: They thought the fire would cover their crime. But one tiny clue led to a trio of killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was shot to death in her Connecticut driveway. Now police must determine if love had turned deadly.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fireman died from what appeared to be heart disease, but his family was skeptical of the cause of death.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2005, David Castor suffered a slow, agonizing death over a period of days. His wife maintained it was suicide, even though it was done with antifreeze.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1986, Gary Dale Larson was stabbed to death in his Edmond, Oklahoma home and then the killer sexually assaulted Larson's girlfriend Janet Haynes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Kathy Lorick was murdered on a Concord, California jogging trail while talking to her husband on her phone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, until a newly formed cold case unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Kelly Eckart's car was found, with her belongings still in it, after she worked a late shift in Franklin, Indiana. Days later, her body was found in an isolated ravine.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man's story of a killing contradicted physical evidence, investigators turned to forensic science.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl ran and hid when she saw her grandmother being beaten to death, but the man followed, beat and assaulted her. She said the assailant was her uncle, who was convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, teenager Tara Munsey went missing after a work shift at a Virginia restaurant. Did she run away, or was she the victim of foul play?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The crime scene was especially violent: A couple had been shot to death. Was their teenage daughter complicit?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why a scientist is gravely ill. When tests reveal the cause, it's too late to save him.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Fifteen years later, DNA technology leads to justice for a murdered woman and her savagely beaten 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who was known to have suffered from depression took her own life. But her sister told police that she said if anything were to happen to her there would be a note in the china cabinet.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An assistant manager of a Florida steakhouse is stabbed to death. It appears to be a robbery gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
